description: The abstract base class for all materials.

maps.purpose: Sets the base color texture of the surface.
Controls surface transparency via grayscale values.

description: A simple material for drawing geometries in a flat or wireframe way.

maps.purpose: Sets the base color texture of the surface.
Controls surface transparency via grayscale values.
Specifies ambient occlusion to fake soft shadowing.
Defines surface reflectivity and shininess placement.

description: A material for non-shiny surfaces, calculated using a Gouraud shading model.

maps.purpose: Sets the base color texture of the surface.
Controls surface transparency via grayscale values.
Grayscale shadow map for non-shiny, diffuse surfaces.
Controls where specular highlights appear under light.
Bakes pre-rendered lighting data directly onto the mesh.

description: A material for shiny surfaces with specular highlights, calculated using a Blinn-Phong model.

maps.purpose: Sets the base color texture of the surface.
Controls surface transparency via grayscale values.
Uses grayscale displacement to fake surface depth.
Modifies vertex normals using RGB data for detailed relief.
Physically deforms vertex positions using height values.
Pinpoints where shiny highlights react to light sources.
Adds local shadows to deep crevices.
Incorporates static environment lighting maps.
Sets the base color texture of the surface.
Controls surface transparency via grayscale values.
Simulates superficial surface roughness details.
Enhances lighting response across micro-details.
Modifies the actual 3D silhouette geometry.
Grayscale layout where black is smooth and white is rough.
Grayscale layout separating metal from dielectric surfaces.
Darkens cracks and occlusion points under ambient light.
Blends baked light maps into the PBR shader calculation.
Defines parts of the mesh that emit light independently.

description: An extension of MeshStandardMaterial, providing advanced physically-based rendering properties.

maps.purpose: Sets the base color texture of the surface.
Controls surface transparency via grayscale values.
Simulates superficial surface roughness details.
Enhances lighting response across micro-details.
Modifies the actual 3D silhouette geometry.
Grayscale layout where black is smooth and white is rough.
Grayscale layout separating metal from dielectric surfaces.
Darkens cracks and occlusion points under ambient light.
Blends baked light maps into the PBR shader calculation.
Defines parts of the mesh that emit light independently.
Adds a shiny outer varnish layer map.
Defines detailed micro-scratches on the outer varnish layer.
Dictates local fuzziness of the clearcoat layer reflection.
Maps cloth-like velvet reflection highlights.
Controls edge fuzziness on cloth and fabric structures.
Manages thin-surface transparency and light refraction.
Dictates surface thickness for subsurface refraction volume.
Maps anti-reflective or thin-film soap-bubble color shifts.

description: An efficient material defined by an encoded Material Capture (MatCap) image.

maps.purpose: Encodes entire lighting, shading, and color spaces into a sphere map.

description: A material implementing a toon/cell shading look.

maps.purpose: Sets the base color texture of the surface.
Controls surface transparency via grayscale values.
Dictates a stepping layout for cell-shaded/anime visuals.
Handles line shading distortions across surface details.
Handles micro-surface variations for step-shading distortion.

description: The default material used by Points objects to render particle-like vertex dots.

maps.purpose: Sets the texture applied to each individual point element.

description: A specialized material designed specifically for 2D sprites that always face the camera.

maps.purpose: Sets the color texture displayed on the sprite face.
Controls sprite transparency boundaries.

description: A highly customizable material rendered with custom GLSL vertex and fragment shaders.

maps.purpose: Accepts custom textures mapped manually inside shader variables.

description: Maps the vertex normal vectors directly to RGB colors for technical diagnostic views.
Renders a mesh by translating depth values into grayscale, where closer geometry is white and farther is black.
A material used for drawing simple non-shaded wireframe lines.
A material used for drawing dashed wireframe lines by defining dash and gap sizes.
A special invisible material that can receive shadows, turning shadow zones into dark alpha values over real-world or background layers.
An unmanaged variant of ShaderMaterial that bypasses built-in uniform and attribute inclusions.
